link_type_list.description: This describes a situation where one label is (or was) a subsidiary of another label, during a given period of time. link_type_list.description:
This describes a situation where one label is reissuing, under its own name, (part of) another label's catalog. This can happen in at least three cases:
  • A label acquires a lease on another label's catalog, for a period of time, in a specific region of the world.
  • A label buys the rights to a defunct label's catalog, or buys a label (with its catalog) and dismantles it.
  • A bootleg label reissues another label's catalog.

link_type_list.description: This describes a situation where a label has changed its name, either for purely aesthetic reasons or following a buyout/sellout/spin-off. link_type_list.description:
This is used to link a karaoke version of a song to the original song.

A karaoke version is a version of the song with the main vocals removed, designed to be used for karaoke. These are generally produced from the original masters by muting the main vocal track or by using post-processing filters to remove the vocals. Karaoke versions can be found labelled in numerous different ways other than "karaoke": instrumental (even if backing vocals are still present), off vocal, backing track, etc.
link_type_list.name: karaoke

link_type_list.description: This indicates that a recording is a compilation of several other recordings. This applies to one long recording that contains multiple songs, one after the other, in which the audio material of the original recordings has not been altered. If the tracks are pitched or blended into each other, the DJ-mix relationship type may be more appropriate.
link_type_list.name: compilation

link_type_list.description: This is used to indicate that the recording is a mash-up of two (or more) other recordings.

link_type_list.description: This links an edit to its original recording. An "edit", for this relationship, can be a radio edit (which involves streamlining a longer track to around the 3 minute mark in order to make it suitable for radio play), or a shortened, censored, or otherwise edited version of the same material. The person who edited the recording can be linked using the editor relationship type.

link_type_list.description: This links a series (usually, but not exclusively, a festival or concert series) to a site where the organisers/creators can receive donations/patronage, such as Flattr or PayPal.me.
link_type_list.name: patronage

link_type_list.description: This links a series to its profile (e.g. for a festival) or project (e.g. for a specific tour, or for compiling a classical catalogue) at a crowdfunding site like Kickstarter or Indiegogo.
link_type_list.name: crowdfunding

link_type_list.description: This links an entity to the equivalent entry in another database. Please respect the whitelist.
link_type_list.long_link_phrase: has a page in a database at
link_type_list.name: other databases

link_type_list.description: Indicates that the work is part of a series.
link_type_list.name: part of

link_type_list.description: This indicates that (most commonly) a festival commissioned the work. This is most common for classical works, but also exists in other genres to a degree.
link_type_list.name: commissioned

link_type_list.description: This relationship describes a URL where lyrics for the work can be found. Only sites on the whitelist are permitted.
link_type_list.name: lyrics

link_type_list.description: Points to the Wikipedia page for this work.
link_type_list.name: wikipedia

link_type_list.description: This is used to link a work to its corresponding page in the SecondHandSongs database.
link_type_list.name: secondhandsongs

link_type_list.description: This is used to link a work to its corresponding page on Allmusic.
link_type_list.name: allmusic

link_type_list.description: This links an entity to the appropriate listing in the Songfacts database, a user contributed database concerned with the stories behind the songs.
link_type_list.name: songfacts

link_type_list.description:
This points to the VIAF page for this work. VIAF is an international project to make a common authority file available to libraries across the world. An authority file is similar to an MBID for libraries. (more information on Wikipedia)

Note: Works in VIAF aren't very detailed. Only add links to MusicBrainz works if you're absolutely sure it's the same work.
link_type_list.name: VIAF

link_type_list.description: Points to the Wikidata page for this work, and will be used to fetch Wikipedia summaries
link_type_list.name: wikidata

link_type_list.description: This links a soundtrack work to the IMDb page for the movie, show or game of which it is a soundtrack.
link_type_list.name: IMDb

link_type_list.description: Points to the BookBrainz page for this work.
link_type_list.name: BookBrainz

link_type_list.description: This relationship type is used to link to a page where the score / sheet music for this work can be legally purchased for mail order.
link_type_list.link_phrase: mail-order purchase page for the score of
link_type_list.long_link_phrase: The score for {entity1} can be purchased for mail-order at {entity0}
link_type_list.name: purchase for mail-order

link_type_list.description: This links a work to a license under which it is available.
link_type_list.name: license

link_type_list.description: This is used to link the Discogs page for this work.
link_type_list.name: discogs

link_type_list.description: This links a soundtrack work to the VGMdb page for the movie, show or game of which it is a soundtrack. VGMdb is a community project dedicated to cataloguing the music of video games and anime.
link_type_list.name: vgmdb

link_type_list.description: This relationship type is used to link to a page where the score / sheet music for this work can be legally purchased for download.
link_type_list.link_phrase: download purchase page for the score of
link_type_list.long_link_phrase: The score for {entity1} can be purchased for download at {entity0}
link_type_list.name: purchase for download

link_type_list.description: This link points to a page for a particular work within a work list for an artist.
link_type_list.long_link_phrase: {entity1} has a work list entry at {entity0}
link_type_list.name: work list entry

link_type_list.description: This relationship type is used to link to a page where the score / sheet music for this work can be legally downloaded for free.
link_type_list.link_phrase: free download page for the score of
link_type_list.long_link_phrase: The score for {entity1} can be downloaded for free at {entity0}
link_type_list.name: download for free

link_type_list.description: This links a work to its project at a crowdfunding site like Kickstarter or Indiegogo.
link_type_list.name: crowdfunding

link_type_list.description: This links an entity to the equivalent entry in another database. Please respect the whitelist.
link_type_list.long_link_phrase: {entity1} has a page in a database at {entity0}
link_type_list.name: other databases

link_type_list.description: This is used to indicate that a work is a medley of several other songs. This means that the original songs were rearranged to create a new work in the form of a medley. See arranger for crediting the person who arranges songs into a medley.
link_type_list.name: medley

link_type_list.description: This links two versions of a work.
link_type_list.name: other version

link_type_list.description: This indicates that a work is made up of multiple parts (e.g. an orchestral suite broken into movements)
link_type_list.name: parts

link_type_list.description: This links two works, where the second work is based on music or text from the first, but isn't directly a revision or an arrangement of it.
link_type_list.name: based on

link_type_list.description: This links different revisions of the same work.
link_type_list.name: revision of

link_type_list.description: This links two works where one work is an orchestration of the other.
link_type_list.name: orchestration

link_type_list.description: This links two works where one work is an arrangement of the other.
link_type_list.name: arrangement

link_type_list.description: This is used when a work includes a brief quotation of the music of another work. In most cases the quotation is uncredited, although this is not a requirement. For a work that includes significantly more of another than just a brief quotation, consider using “based on” instead.
link_type_list.name: musical quotation

link_type_list.description: This is used when a work includes a brief quotation of the lyrics of another work. In most cases the quotation is uncredited, although this is not a requirement. For a work that includes significantly more of another than just a brief quotation, consider using “based on” instead.
link_type_list.name: lyrical quotation
